name: splitflap-display
title: Split-Flap Display
author: Cody Shanley
description: Transitions caller-selected text through a deterministic mechanical split-flap alphabet.

changes: Imported the colocated stylesheet, removed random selection and autonomous cycling, and made refreshSignal the only word-advance trigger.
Made the initial word deterministic and live announcements opt-in after all visible slats settle.
Added a settled callback, snapped reduced-motion and unsupported-character transitions, and paused the active timeout while the document is hidden.
Replaced the timer list with one visibility-aware pending timer, synchronized the current-character ref so rapid targets cancel and restart safely, and corrected the upstream distance example.
Added a reusable visually-hidden text class and bounded wide displays with horizontal overflow while preventing slat shrinkage.

modifications.change: Removed random and automatic word selection, retained refreshSignal as the explicit sequential control, imported the CSS, and delayed optional live text until every slat settles.
modifications.reason: Keep state changes caller-controlled, avoid unattended motion, and announce only the value actually shown.

modifications.change: Added settled reporting, unsupported-character and reduced-motion snapping, document-visibility pausing, and single-timer cancellation.
modifications.reason: Preserve visible text outside the mechanical alphabet and prevent hidden, stale, or post-unmount animation work.

modifications.change: Added the semantic text utility and responsive horizontal overflow containment.
modifications.reason: Expose one nonvisual phrase and keep fixed mechanical slats usable in narrow containers.
